note: Each bug is the exact reverse of a real, merged Gin bug-fix, the regression that actually shipped before maintainers patched it. BUG_001 reverses the fix for CVE-2023-29401 (Content-Disposition filename header injection). No synthetic bugs; every bug links its upstream fix commit (and PR).

bugs.severity_note: Recalibrated high->medium to match the official rating (CVSS 4.3, medium) for NVD CVE-2023-29401. The provenance (a real, assigned CVE) is the point, not an inflated severity.
bugs.description: Reverts the fix for CVE-2023-29401. In FileAttachment the ASCII branch interpolates the caller-supplied filename straight into the Content-Disposition header value without escaping quotes/backslashes. An attacker-controlled filename containing a double-quote (or a backslash before a backquote) breaks out of the quoted filename and injects additional header-field content. The real fix routed the filename through escapeQuotes(); reverting drops that call so the raw filename is concatenated again. A real, assigned CVE about response-header injection, reintroduced.

Reverts the nil-guard in initQueryCache. The guard checked only `c.Request != nil` before calling `c.Request.URL.Query()`; if a request is constructed with a nil URL (common in unit tests and some middleware that build *http.Request directly), `c.Request.URL.Query()` dereferences a nil pointer and panics, taking down the handler. The real fix added `&& c.Request.URL != nil`. Reverting drops it, reintroducing the nil-pointer panic.

injection.find:
if c.queryCache == nil {
		if c.Request != nil && c.Request.URL != nil {
injection.replace_with:
if c.queryCache == nil {
		if c.Request != nil {

bugs.description: Reverts the bounds fix in NegotiateFormat. The loop compares accepted[i] and offer[i] but only bounded i by len(accepted); when an offered MIME type is shorter than the accepted one, offer[i] reads past the end of the string slice and panics with index out of range, an attacker-influenceable DoS via the Accept header. The real fix added `&& i < len(offer)` to the loop condition. Reverting drops it, reintroducing the out-of-bounds panic.

injection.find:
i := 0
			for ; i < len(accepted) && i < len(offer); i++ {

bugs.description: Reverts the resource-leak fix in RunFd. After wrapping the inherited file descriptor with os.NewFile, the code passes it to net.FileListener but never closes the *os.File, leaking a file descriptor on every RunFd call (descriptor exhaustion over a process's lifetime). The real fix added `defer f.Close()` immediately after os.NewFile. Reverting removes that defer, reintroducing the leak.

injection.find:
f := os.NewFile(uintptr(fd), fmt.Sprintf("fd@%d", fd))
	defer f.Close()
	listener, err := net.FileListener(f)
injection.replace_with:
f := os.NewFile(uintptr(fd), fmt.Sprintf("fd@%d", fd))
	listener, err := net.FileListener(f)
